findings.message: subprocess.run(command, shell=True) passes LLM-generated command string directly to shell; same pattern present in all 13 agent files (s01-s12, s_full) and 2 reference scripts
Blocklist ['rm -rf /', 'sudo', 'shutdown', 'reboot', '> /dev/'] is trivially bypassed by trailing space, uppercase variants, or indirect paths; present in all agent run_bash() implementations
anthropic>=0.25.0, python-dotenv>=1.0.0, pyyaml>=6.0 allow unbounded major-version upgrades on each fresh pip install, creating supply-chain and breaking-change risk
Skill provides agent design guidance but declares no response template or structure for how the LLM should format its output when the skill is active
'Load it when relevant' does not specify what conditions make knowledge relevant; reader cannot act on this without criteria
'Perform thorough code reviews' in the description does not define what thoroughness requires; checklist body provides criteria but the descriptor is still undefined
'conducting comprehensive code reviews' provides no specifics; the subsequent checklist defines scope but the framing word is still vague
'Meaningful, specific checks' uses 'Meaningful' without defining what separates a meaningful assertion from a trivial one
MCP builder skill provides templates and best practices but no guidance on how the LLM should structure its response when helping a user build an MCP server
'Return meaningful error messages' does not define what makes an error message meaningful (include error code? user action? context?)
'Never expose sensitive operations without auth' does not define which operations are sensitive; readers cannot apply this without a criteria list
'Tools should be safe to retry' does not define what safety means in this context (idempotent? no side effects? limited blast radius?)
PDF skill teaches workflows and libraries but provides no template for how the LLM should structure its reply when applying the skill
'(preferred)' labels Option 1 as preferred without stating why; reader cannot make an informed choice in edge cases
'(recommended)' for Markdown-to-PDF conversion is stated without criteria; reader cannot evaluate when to deviate
'PDFs may contain various character encodings' — 'various' is a placeholder; adds no actionable information
All npm dependencies use ^ ranges; minor/patch updates can introduce regressions in a deployed Next.js app
